candidates.why: Represents the primary operating revenue line; best match for total revenue/sales.

candidates.why: Investment income, not core operating revenue; less appropriate than Operating Revenue.

This is a cost line, not revenue.
Finance income category, not top-line revenue.
Specific interest income, not operating revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Net income measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income / (loss) for the period
candidates.why: Bottom-line comprehensive income, not revenue.

variables.notes: Operating Revenue is the clear top-line revenue measure; other candidates are income or cost items, not sales.

candidates.row_label: Cost of Goods and Services Sold
candidates.why: Standard label for cost of goods/services; matches COGS definition.
Explicitly labeled as COGS; also a strong candidate. Either could be used depending on reporting, but this is slightly more generic to all revenues.
Overhead/SG&A-type costs rather than direct COGS.
Likely miscellaneous operating expenses, not specifically COGS.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Classified within SG&A, not COGS.
Finance/transaction charges, not production/service costs.
Net non-operating result, not COGS.
Capitalised asset costs, not period expense COGS.
Liability balance, not an income statement COGS line.

variables.notes: Both 'Cost of Goods and Services Sold' and 'Cost of revenues/COGS' are COGS-type lines; the explicitly labeled 'Cost of revenues/COGS' is chosen as the primary COGS measure.

candidates.why: Broad operating/admin expense bucket; classic SG&A-type overhead.
Personnel-related overhead, part of SG&A.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Stock-based comp explicitly within SG&A; should be included as a component.

final_choice.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses

variables.notes: Selected multiple SG&A-related component rows (admin & operating, salaries/benefits, SG&A stock-based comp) and excluded totals/balancing and non-SG&A items.
No research & development expense line (or similar) appears in the provided Income Statement or Balance Sheet previews, so XRD is treated as not separately reported (set to 0).

candidates.why: Balance sheet asset, not an interest expense line.

candidates.row_label: Interest income from cash balance at amortised cost
candidates.why: Clearly labeled as interest income, not expense.
Also an interest income line, not expense.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. No explicit 'Interest expense' line is present. The best proxies for interest/finance costs are 'Sundry finance expenses' and 'Bank charge Expense', which likely capture net finance/interest-related expenses. Selected these from the full Income Statement, outside the candidate list; review recommended to confirm they represent interest expense rather than miscellaneous fees. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Sundry finance expenses, [Income Statement] Bank charge Expense

candidates.why: Component of equity, but not total book equity.
Represents total shareholders’ equity; appropriate for BE when no separate parent/owner equity vs NCI breakdown is provided.
Represents total assets/liabilities plus equity; not an equity-only measure.

variables.notes: No separate line for equity attributable to owners vs non-controlling interests; 'Total Equity' is used as book equity, and no MIB is mapped so BE will not double count.
No line for minority interest or non-controlling interests appears on the Balance Sheet; MIB is set to 0.
